steps.title: Identify the grammar issue
steps.content: The passage describes a general, ongoing situation (Perl and Pascal "have" a difference; both languages "require" technical knowledge). The verb tense must stay consistent with this present-tense frame. The subject "laypeople" is plural.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A: struggle — plural present tense. "Laypeople struggle to distinguish between them" — matches the present-tense context and agrees with the plural subject. Correct.

B: struggled — simple past. Shifts the sentence into the past, contradicting the present-tense framing of the passage. Eliminated.

C: were struggling — past progressive. Implies a completed past action, inconsistent with the general present-tense claim. Eliminated.

D: had struggled — past perfect. Implies the struggling was completed before another past event, which is not supported by the passage. Eliminated.
